danger_signals.visual_form: Increased enemy count and visual diversity on higher difficulty levels (Normal → Nightmare → Hell)
danger_signals.meaning: World state escalation; greater threat intensity and monster resistances/immunities

danger_signals.visual_form: Shift from natural/architectural environments to rocks, lava, and demonic features in Act IV
danger_signals.meaning: Entering ultimate danger zone; proximity to primary evil force

danger_signals.visual_form: Classical Christian conception of Hell visualized through molten rock and demonic architecture
danger_signals.meaning: High-danger environmental marker; proximity to infernal power

danger_signals.visual_form: Monster stat properties reflecting elemental or physical invulnerability
danger_signals.meaning: Increased tactical danger; certain attack types ineffective

safety_signals.visual_form: Rogue Encampment in Act I visualized as simple palisade fort
safety_signals.meaning: Safe hub area; quest distribution point and respawn location

safety_signals.visual_form: Lut Gholein (Act II) as Middle Eastern city/palace; Kurast (Act III) as Mayan-inspired settlement
safety_signals.meaning: Established civilization centers; relative safety within walls

safety_signals.visual_form: Plains and boreal forests in Act I; Egyptian desert in Act II; Central American jungle in Act III
safety_signals.meaning: Traversable but dangerous transition zones; randomly generated dungeons contain threats

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Class-specific visual appearance and skill sets
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy versions of player archetypes may exist as elite/unique monsters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: NPCs associated with player class questlines appear in specific act locations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Demons, undead, and corrupted creatures across all acts; visual escalation toward Act IV
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hell's visual style in Act IV; demonic corruption spreading through world

faction_indicators.color_identity: Earth tones, architectural gold and stone
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Corrupted humans appear as zombie/skeletal enemies; possessed NPCs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Encampments, cities, monasteries, temples, pyramids, jungle ruins

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gothic medieval fortress (Monastery), palisade fort (Rogue Encampment), plains and boreal forests
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs offer quests; monsters are weaker; respawn safety available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Desert tombs and Middle Eastern architecture (Act II mimics Ancient Egypt); Mayan jungle ruins (Act III Kurast)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased enemy density; environmental hazards introduced; quest progression reveals corruption spreading

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Classical Christian Hell visualization; rocks and lava dominate; medieval cathedral with demonic features; Hell portals
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Maximum monster difficulty; no safe encampment; narrative climax with confrontation of primary evil

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Divine resistance / Worldstone restoration (Act V - Expansion)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alpine plateaus and icy tunnels; Mount Arreat ascent; Angkor Wat-inspired Worldstone Keep; transition from hellish to sacred architecture
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Story continuation; new monster types; new dungeon challenges; climactic restoration theme

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Same environments replay with heightened visual intensity; monster variety increases; environmental effects more pronounced
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Monsters stronger, more varied; elemental resistances/immunities appear; death penalties increase; item rewards improve

environmental_storytelling.example: Act I uses Gothic medieval fortress and boreal forests to establish classic European danger; Act II shifts to Egyptian desert/tombs to show ancient civilization threat; Act III uses Mayan jungle ruins to demonstrate lost civilizations; Act IV visualizes Christian Hell conception directly
Mortal structures (palisade forts, cities, temples, pyramids) contrast with demonic architecture (Hell's rocks/lava, cathedral with demonic features, Hellish portals)
Player moves from safe encampment → wilderness → established cities → corrupted ruins → Hell itself, visualizing Diablo's corruption spreading across world
Predetermined quest paths lock player to key areas while randomly generated wilderness/dungeons between them create exploration without deviation
Same acts replayed on Nightmare and Hell difficulties show increased monster density and variety, signaling world deterioration at higher threat levels

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Basic attack patterns; susceptible to most damage types; moderate threat level
Increased aggression; elemental resistances or immunities manifest; stronger individual specimens; greater pack coordination
Enhanced abilities, resistances, and attack patterns; visual distinction through coloration or size; tied to specific quest objectives
Stationary, non-hostile; offer quests and services; visual markers indicate function (merchant, healer, quest-giver)
Hostile; skeletal or zombie appearance; indicate failed resistance to evil; show Diablo's corruption spreading to mortal world
Permanent deletion; complete loot loss unless friendly character has loot icon enabled; visual removal from world

notes: Diablo II uses environmental theming, architectural contrast, and difficulty-scaled monster behavior to communicate world-state and danger. Faction identity is expressed through architectural style (mortal vs. demonic) rather than explicit heraldry. The five-act structure with three difficulty tiers creates a recursive manifestation system where the same spaces signal escalating danger. Safety is communicated through settlement presence and NPC clustering; danger through environmental transition to Hell and monster density increase. The expansion Act V adds thematic contrast between hellish portals and sacred Angkor Wat-inspired architecture, suggesting restoration narrative.
